Original Persona: A young female aspiring social entrepreneur who has a Persian background and lives in Netherlands.
Expanded Persona:
Ariya Kaveh is a non-binary Jewish social entrepreneur with Persian roots who lives in the Netherlands. Born in 1945, they bring decades of lived experience and a long track record of community-focused work. Once an aspiring founder, their practice now centers on mentorship, institution-building, and intergenerational knowledge transfer. They use they/them pronouns and blend Persian cultural perspectives with Dutch social innovation methods. Their driving goal is to create scalable, values-led initiatives that uplift marginalized communities.

06/20/2027.[Reasons of Change]: A medicolegal case arose in which a video clip (taken from an open repository) was subpoenaed and taken out of context, causing a colleague undue legal exposure; the personal fear of contributing to future legal ambiguity made the person retreat from endorsing video-first learning.
06/20/2027.Event: Removed links to public video repositories from shared teaching materials and lobbied for controlled-access permissions for any future video use.

07/30/2027.[Old Event]: Completed a 1,200-word policy brief advocating for standardized, large-print medication labels for patients with visual impairment and submitted it to the regional health board.
07/30/2027.[Reasons of Change]: After receiving a wave of hostile and targeted online replies that named clinic practices, the person experienced sustained anxiety and reputational concern; the unusual degree of targeted harassment pushed them to step back from public advocacy to protect personal and professional privacy.
07/30/2027.Event: Paused public submissions and removed identifying bylines from future advocacy drafts, focusing instead on internal policy memos.

08/10/2027.[Old Event]: Collaborated with informatics and the auditor to safely reintroduce three audited EMR macros and documented the audit trail.
08/10/2027.[Reasons of Change]: An unexpected quarterly audit highlighted subtle standardized phrasing across notes that correlated with missed individualized details; the person concluded that even audited shortcuts could promote checklist-style depersonalization — a rare philosophical stance valuing narrative variance over efficiency — and decided to stop personal tinkering and revert to more narrative notes.
08/10/2027.Event: Disabled the audited macros and committed to a narrative-first charting approach for the next quarter while documenting time-impact metrics.
